requirements.boundary: Local session auth and RBAC are implemented for private beta dry-runs; production identity review remains external.
requirements.name: Identity, organization, permissions

requirements.boundary: Packet intake creates review state, not import approval.
requirements.name: Packet intake

requirements.boundary: No evidence, no claim; stale/reference-only evidence blocks external claims.
requirements.name: Evidence ledger

requirements.boundary: Refresh records are evidence inputs; they do not replace qualified review.
requirements.name: Official source refresh

requirements.boundary: External-world claims stay blocked until evidence and human gates close.
requirements.name: Claim and blocker engine

requirements.boundary: Router controls local simulated review and endpoint contracts; no live provider call is made by default.
requirements.name: AI processing and model routing

requirements.boundary: AI reviewers create findings and next moves only; they cannot open gates.
requirements.name: AI simulated reviewers

requirements.boundary: Operator UX is local/private-beta oriented.
requirements.name: Operator UX

requirements.boundary: Expert review packet is scoped; no advice or approval is implied by the app.
requirements.name: Expert review UX

requirements.boundary: Reports disclose blockers and AI involvement; they are not certificates.
requirements.name: Reports and exports

requirements.boundary: Customer UX is private-beta candidate, not public launch proof.
requirements.name: Customer UX

requirements.boundary: Admin controls are local/private-beta implementation surfaces.
requirements.name: Admin UX

requirements.boundary: Policies and deletion workflow exist; qualified privacy/legal review remains required before hosted customer use.
requirements.name: Privacy and data controls

requirements.boundary: Production identity, TLS, secret manager, malware scanning, and security review remain external gates.
requirements.name: Security

requirements.boundary: Local JSON/SQLite audit exists; production observability stack remains a deployment gate.
requirements.name: Audit and observability

requirements.boundary: Hostable artifacts exist; no live production environment is claimed.
requirements.name: Deployment environments

requirements.boundary: Acceptance proves local product behavior and generated artifacts, not external legal/compliance approval.
requirements.name: Testing and acceptance gates

requirements.boundary: Public quick check is no-login draft PDF generation with delete controls; it is not approval, advice, shipment readiness, or persistent account history.
requirements.name: Public trade readiness quick check

requirements.boundary: Exporter packet creates evidence gaps and review packets only.
requirements.name: Create Export-to-Canada packet

requirements.boundary: Missing buyer/importer creates a blocker; presence is not buyer validation.
requirements.name: Identify Canadian buyer/importer

requirements.boundary: Incoterms classify responsibility path only; they do not prove legal or customs readiness.
requirements.name: Capture Incoterms or delivery responsibility

requirements.boundary: Exporter/DDP/non-resident importer responsibility triggers broker review gates.
requirements.name: Mark Canadian import responsibility

requirements.boundary: Canadian official/reference checks remain evidence only until qualified review.
requirements.name: Check Canadian-side readiness blockers

requirements.boundary: Exporter-side readiness is a document-gap lane, not country legal advice.
requirements.name: Check exporter-side document readiness

requirements.boundary: Buyer packet is a draft question/evidence packet and cannot claim buyer validation.
requirements.name: Generate buyer-ready packet

requirements.boundary: Broker packet asks for scoped review; it is not broker advice or clearance.
requirements.name: Generate Canadian broker-review packet

requirements.boundary: All external-world claims remain blocked without qualified evidence and human gates.
requirements.name: Block compliance, tariff, CFIA, buyer, and shipment claims

requirements.boundary: Starter mode creates a missing-evidence packet and checklist; it is not advice or approval.
requirements.name: Beginner no-documents starter flow

requirements.boundary: PDF triage detects local text/OCR needs and asks for confirmation; it does not verify document authenticity.
requirements.name: PDF triage and native text extraction decision

requirements.boundary: User confirmation records draft context only; human experts still own external claims.
requirements.name: User confirmation before extracted fields become packet context

requirements.boundary: The contract maps sources, snapshots, diffs, and packet impacts; live source truth and qualified review remain external gates.
requirements.name: Intelligence Hub policy/source monitoring contract

requirements.boundary: Opportunity rows are signals and research prompts, not recommendations, demand proof, margin proof, or buyer validation.
requirements.name: Opportunity scanner with research gates

requirements.boundary: Coverage tiers describe product support level only; they are not country-specific legal, customs, tariff, or compliance correctness.
requirements.name: Country coverage tiers

requirements.boundary: Transport readiness prepares freight-forwarder questions; it does not guarantee routes, costs, carrier choice, or shipment readiness.
requirements.name: Transport readiness lane

requirements.boundary: Billing controls define plan, credit, and heavy-job authorization only; live checkout and invoicing are disabled.
requirements.name: Billing and credit controls

requirements.boundary: Agents can create packets and reports through product gates only; no live public API gateway or claim-opening tool is exposed.
requirements.name: Agent/API contract

requirements.boundary: Traffic/checklist pages educate and route to the tool; they are not legal, customs, tariff, supplier, buyer, or shipment advice.
requirements.name: Traffic-first public pages

requirements.boundary: All locally implementable stages have routes, APIs, and artifacts; real-world approvals remain external gates.
requirements.name: All local product stages

requirements.boundary: Research routing chooses model-prior, web, official-source, dataset, and expert/user lanes; it does not prove the external facts.
requirements.name: Research and data execution routing

requirements.boundary: The product can prepare and route scoped expert packets; real qualified findings remain external evidence.
requirements.name: Expert network and review routing

requirements.boundary: Team workspace roles and approval boards are local coordination surfaces, not real owner approval.
requirements.name: Business/team workspace

requirements.boundary: Gateway tools execute as local dry-run contracts only; no public API, credentials, payments, or claim-opening authority are exposed.
requirements.name: Agent API gateway dry-run

requirements.boundary: Launch operations are ready for private-beta review; production deployment and public launch require owner approval.
requirements.name: Launch operations and private beta controls
